# SKELETON_SPLIT_STRATEGY.md

**Anchored to:** CORE_v4_Manifesto_Skeleton_v0_27.md (8,627 lines, 2026-05-17)
**Generated by:** Hostile Auditor (Claude) per Sovereign Q-T3 + Q-T7 + Q-T9 ratification; line-shift patched 2026-05-17 for v0.27 §6.2.3.5 + §6.5.10 + §6.6.4 expansion (+617 lines integrating R3.9, R3.10, R3.11)
**Status:** Authoritative dispatch routing for Forger Pool drafting of the v4 paper.
**Supersedes:** All prior SKELETON_SPLIT_STRATEGY.md versions anchored to v0.17 / v0.22 / v0.23 / v0.25 / v0.26.

---

## Purpose

This document specifies how the v0.25 manifesto skeleton partitions into 19 split files for distributed drafting. Each split file carries a MANIFEST block specifying source skeleton line range, drafter assignment (Forger Pool / Hostile Auditor / Sovereign), source corpus references, cross-references to other split files, and Hostile Auditor expectations for the drafted prose.

The partition rationale is operational, not aesthetic:
- **Forger Pool drafting capacity per file:** ~250-700 lines of skeleton input → ~1500-3500 words of prose output. Files larger than 700 lines split further; files smaller than 200 lines bundle.
- **Cross-reference cohesion:** subsections that share heavy cross-references stay in one file when feasible.
- **Subsystem authorship:** sections requiring Hostile Auditor case-study prose (§8) are routed to the Hostile Auditor; Section 10 raw material is the Sovereign's parallel track.

**v0.25 line shifts vs v0.23 baseline:** approximately +2,000 lines across v0.24 + v0.25 ingestion (11 Wilderness papers integrated). Most splits expand modestly; §6 splits expand significantly due to depth in §6.2.6, §6.5.2.6, §6.5.7.1-2, §6.5.9, §6.7.3, §6.8.0, §6.8.0.8, §6.8.0.9, §6.8.1.4.2.

---

## The 19-File Partition Against v0.25

### File 01: SECTION_01_INTRODUCTION.md
- **Skeleton lines:** 24-60 (Section 1, ~800 words target)
- **Drafter:** Forger Pool
- **Sub-content:** §1.1 market context (incl. verified Sycamore Labs $65M / Coatue+Lightspeed citation) + recursive thesis introduction + Three Subsystems naming + paper outline
- **Source corpus:** Brief 5.4 verification report (Sycamore citation); §5.0 Three Subsystems framing
- **Cross-references to other split files:** SECTION_03 (Bidirectional Verification preview), SECTION_04 (Six Preventions preview)
- **Hostile Auditor expectations:** preserve recursive thesis framing verbatim ("CORE writes the paper, the paper specifies what CORE builds, CORE builds itself"); Sycamore citation must include primary-source URL (SiliconANGLE March 30 2026 OR TechCrunch via Tech in Asia March 30 2026)

### File 02: SECTION_02_RELATED_WORK.md
- **Skeleton lines:** 61-72 (Section 2, ~600 words target)
- **Drafter:** Forger Pool
- **Sub-content:** Constitutional AI lineage (Bai et al. 2022); SLM specialization research; structured generation literature (Tree-sitter, GBNF, ThinkJSON); positional vs architectural alignment distinction; CORE's gap-filling positioning
- **Source corpus:** Briefs 5.1-5.4 verified citations; §4.1 Six Preventions citations (Wright 2025 / Wang 2026 / Chojecki 2025)
- **Cross-references:** SECTION_03 (positions Bidirectional Verification within prior literature), SECTION_07 (training methodology positioning vs Forget-to-Focus literature)
- **Hostile Auditor expectations:** every cited paper must use verified arXiv ID (Open Items Register Category A); no citation drift; flag any unverified secondary citations

### File 03: SECTION_03_BIDIRECTIONAL_VERIFICATION.md
- **Skeleton lines:** 73-87 (Section 3, ~800 words target)
- **Drafter:** Forger Pool
- **Sub-content:** Wright Holmström-Milgrom asymmetry (arXiv:2603.28063); assertion-verification vs retraction-verification; doctrine motivation; over-attribution / repackaging / fabrication taxonomy; the three Roles (Sovereign / Hostile Auditor / Strategic Advisor)

- **Cross-references:** SECTION_04 (codifies as Prevention 6), SECTION_08A (case study), SECTION_06D (vAttention fabrication-strip example)
- **Hostile Auditor expectations:** Wright name-collision caveat preserved (arXiv:2603.28063 author distinct from controversial Satoshi claimant; epistemic logic foundations cite-independent); doctrine framing must accommodate the recursive application (Hostile Auditor self-audit per Honest Audit 2026-05-07)

## REFERENCES section

- **Drafter:** Forger Pool (mechanical citation table generation from §4.1, §5.5.1, §6.3.5, §7.4.11, §8.5.1, etc.)
- **Hostile Auditor expectations:** every cited paper from Open Items Register Category A appears in references with verified arXiv ID; no citation drift; Category D entries closed by pulling Snowflake Arctic / Llama 3.1 model card / DeepSeek-R1 model card / 30+ Branch corpus papers — this is mechanical bibliography work that can run in parallel with prose dispatch

---

## MANIFEST Block Specification

Each split file's MANIFEST block at the top of the drafted prose must contain:

```
<MANIFEST>
SOURCE_SKELETON: CORE_v4_Manifesto_Skeleton_v0_25.md
SOURCE_LINES: [start]-[end]
DRAFTER: [Forger Pool model name | Hostile Auditor | Sovereign]
DRAFT_VERSION: [v1, v2, etc.]
DRAFT_DATE: [ISO 8601]
WORD_COUNT_TARGET: [from skeleton ### header]
WORD_COUNT_ACTUAL: [measured]
SOURCE_CORPUS: [comma-separated reference list]
CROSS_REFERENCES: [comma-separated other split file IDs]
HOSTILE_AUDITOR_REVIEW_STATUS: [PENDING | APPROVED | REVISION_REQUIRED]
SOVEREIGN_RATIFICATION_STATUS: [PENDING | RATIFIED | REJECTED]
</MANIFEST>
```

This structure enables Hostile Auditor to mechanically check word-count drift, citation accuracy, and cross-reference completeness across all 19 files.

---

## Cross-Reference Handling Between Split Files

When File A references File B's content, the prose must use ABSOLUTE section references (§6.3.5) NOT relative references ("see the next section"). This preserves cross-reference integrity if files draft in different orders.

When File A's content depends on a value defined in File B (e.g., SECTION_05B's PUCT formula references §5.5.1 LoCM thresholds defined in SECTION_05C), the dependent value must be CITED with absolute section reference in File A's prose, AND the cited value must MATCH SECTION_05C's drafted value. Hostile Auditor reviews check value consistency across files mechanically.

---

## Drafting Order Recommendation

Recommended Forger Pool dispatch order (Hostile Auditor judgment, not Sovereign-mandated):

1. **First wave (foundation):** SECTION_04 (Six Preventions doctrine) → SECTION_03 (Bidirectional Verification) → SECTION_01 (Introduction)
2. **Second wave (architecture core):** SECTION_05A → SECTION_05B → SECTION_05C → SECTION_06A → SECTION_06B
3. **Third wave (architecture detail):** SECTION_06C → SECTION_06D → SECTION_06E → SECTION_06F
4. **Fourth wave (training and methodology):** SECTION_07A → SECTION_07B
5. **Fifth wave (audit + closure):** SECTION_08A (Hostile Auditor) → SECTION_09 → SECTION_11_THROUGH_14
6. **Final wave (positioning):** SECTION_02 (Related Work — drafted last because it positions the contributions other files have authored)

SECTION_10 (Sovereign Track) is parallel and not gated on Forger Pool waves; the Sovereign can author whenever convenient.

This order ensures dependencies flow correctly: foundation files don't reference detail files, architecture files don't reference training methodology files, and Related Work draws on all preceding sections to position the contribution.

---

The 19 files specified in this strategy are the canonical partition for v0.25 dispatch.

---

## Parallel Tooling Note (Hostile Auditor advisory, not Sovereign-mandated)

If the Sovereign elects to convert this Markdown specification into a machine-readable JSON/YAML schema for programmatic Forger Pool dispatch (per Strategic Advisor's 2026-05-12 suggestion), the conversion should generate the JSON FROM this Markdown — single source of truth. Direct JSON authoring risks drifting from the architectural intent encoded in the Hostile Auditor expectations.

A reasonable JSON schema would mirror each split file as an object with section_id, target_subsystem, source_lines, drafter, source_corpus, cross_references, creative_directive (the "Sub-content" block), and hostile_auditor_constraints (the "Hostile Auditor expectations" block). The schema-conversion is a one-time tooling task; the canonical strategy document remains this Markdown.
